name: Paracas Textile
primary_sources: A History of the World in 100 Objects

visual_attributes.morphology: Large woven cloth with embroidered designs
distinctive_features: Depictions of flying shamans holding severed heads
visual_attributes.texture: Soft and fibrous with intricate embroidery
visual_attributes.size_and_scale: Up to 100 feet (34 meters) in length
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: Large cloths used to wrap mummified bodies

domain_specific_functional.CULTURAL_ROLE: Part of burial customs in Paracas culture

relationships.description: Tello discovered the Paracas necropolis and its textiles in the 1920s.
Some Paracas textiles are preserved and displayed in the British Museum.

contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: Represents the spiritual and ceremonial practices of the Paracas culture
primary_sources: A History of the World in 100 Objects

contrastive_constraints: NOT Inca or later cultural artifact
